mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of periods following an INDPRO decline, or the signal reverses direction during supply-chain normalization phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 800 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of months where HOUST drops below 800.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%, or the interaction effect yields a Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).
